title: Orlando Trip — itinerary planning grid
html:
Orlando Trip — itinerary planning grid

Source: 2021-03-14 Google Doc — pre-trip planning matrix for Yahir's March 22–26, 2021 Orlando trip. Anchor: a Magic vs Nuggets NBA game at Amway Center; surrounding stops include theme parks, an airboat ride through the Everglades fringe, Kennedy Space Center, Clearwater Beach, and a Medieval Times-style dinner.

TL;DR

  • Anchor event — Magic vs Nuggets game at Amway Center (March 22 evening, "7 PM GAME") drives the entire booking — hotels filtered by proximity to Amway.
  • Itinerary grid — 5-day matrix (22–26) crossed with 3-hour time blocks: Day 1 flight + check-in, Day 2 Kennedy Space Center + game, Day 3 airboat ride + Clearwater Beach, Day 4 Universal Studios, Day 5 flight home.
  • Hotel candidates — Grand Bohemian Hotel Orlando (.3mi from Amway), Embassy Suites Orlando Downtown (.5mi) — TripAdvisor-sourced.
  • Bonus list — Gatorland, Topgolf, Epcot, Universal Studios Florida, Escapology — fallback / overflow options.
  • Pre-trip companion to the existing March 13 booking note and March 25 in trip bus route note — the three docs together comprise the full Orlando trip planning record.

Pattern observed

This is a clean instance of Yahir's scrappy travel mode in proto-form — a 17-year-old Grants Pass kid building his own NBA-trip itinerary around a single sports anchor and bus-routing the rest of it (per the in-trip note). The Orlando trip predates the Ha Giang / Shanghai / Austin travel-and-relocate sequence by 3+ years and demonstrates the logistics-first travel planning muscle was already there at 17. Magic-game-as-anchor → hotel-near-arena → fill-the-rest → cheap-transport mirrors the same pattern as the 2024 Valeria Shanghai itinerary.

Open questions

  • Did the Medieval Times dinner happen? (Note has it as "Medieval Dinner?" with a question mark.)
  • Who went on the trip with Yahir? (Orlando entity notes "solo-ish" — no other names appear in any of the three Orlando-trip docs.)
